questions.question: What do artificial neural networks in AI share conceptually with biological neural networks in the brain?
options: Both use electrochemical signals in salt-water channels
Both use layers of interconnected nodes that activate based on weighted input signals
Both update connections through chemical synaptic growth
Both can only operate on carbon-based substrates

questions.question: In neuroscience, a "place cell" is a neuron that fires when an animal is at a specific location. In which brain structure are place cells found?
options: Prefrontal cortex
Hippocampus
Cerebellum
Basal ganglia

questions.question: Modern neuroscience favors "population coding" over the "grandmother cell" hypothesis. What does population coding mean?
options: Individual neurons encode specific concepts, confirming the original hypothesis
Concepts are represented by the combined activity pattern of many neurons, not single cells
Neurons form permanent clusters during early development that represent categories
Memories are stored in DNA methylation patterns, not firing patterns

talks.title: What's in Your Head? Glia, Neuroimmunity & the Blood Brain Barrier
talks.speaker: Kendra Lechtenberg, PhD

questions.question: For decades, textbooks claimed neurons made up 10% of brain cells and glia 90%. What do modern studies actually show?
options: Neurons outnumber glia 9-to-1
Glia and neurons are present in roughly equal numbers
Glia outnumber neurons 90-to-1 as originally claimed
The ratio varies so dramatically by brain region that no overall figure is meaningful

questions.question: Which type of glial cell is responsible for myelinating axons in the central nervous system?
options: Astrocytes
Microglia
Oligodendrocytes
Schwann cells

questions.question: What is the "glymphatic system" and when is it most active?
options: The network of lymph nodes surrounding the spinal cord; active during exercise
A cerebrospinal fluid waste-clearance system in the brain; most active during sleep
A signaling pathway between microglia and peripheral immune cells; active during infection
The active transport layer of the blood-brain barrier; active during stress

originStory.text:
Hi. I'm Justin Fritz — and I'm a Nerd Nite Boss.

That's a real title. The Nerd Nite organization gave it to me, and I take it very seriously.

Nerd Nite started in Boston in 2003. Graduate student Chris Balakrishnan had been telling the same stories at his local bar — The Midway Cafe — about studying parasitic birds in Cameroon. The bartenders finally told him: just give a talk. One night. Slides. Get it out of your system.

He did. It worked.

In 2006, his roommate Matt Wasowski moved the concept to New York City, expanded it beyond science to any topic worth a drink and 20 minutes, and built it into a global brand — the "Discovery Channel with beer" — now in over 100 cities worldwide.

It came to Fort Collins because of me and one other very important person: Senne Van Loon.

But first — trivia.

Senne and I built this thing from scratch.

Senne is the kind of person who hears "let's put on a nerdy event at a bar" and says yes immediately — full commitment, no hesitation. That energy is rare and it matters. You cannot build something like this alone.

As we grew, Jamie Fritz joined us. Jamie is the backbone — always behind the scenes, making everything work, never needing the spotlight. The kind of person every organization needs and rarely thanks enough.

Jamie has been onstage exactly once: to introduce Marybeth Arcodia. Which brings me to Marybeth.

Marybeth Arcodia was our host.

She was brilliant at it — warm, sharp, funny in exactly the right way for a room full of nerds on their third drink. She held the stage like she was born for it.

Marybeth eventually left Fort Collins for a faculty position in Miami. We were sad to lose her and enormously proud of her.

Hannah Bauer stepped up as host after Marybeth — and she's been incredible.

Here's the thing you need to understand about Nerd Nite: once you're a Boss, you're always a Boss. Marybeth is still a Boss. Distance doesn't change that.

You just played Nerdometer.

Every edition is built around a real Nerd Nite Fort Collins event — the actual talks, the actual speakers, the real nerdy obsessions people bring to a bar and share with strangers.

Senne. Jamie. Marybeth. Hannah. Every speaker who ever trusted a room full of people with the thing they love most.

All Bosses. Always.

Come be part of it.

— Justin Fritz, Nerd Nite Fort Collins Boss
